q: Your streaming pipeline joins incoming transactions against a merchants dimension table updated daily. About 2% of records are missing merchant enrichment. Walk through why this happens and how you fix it.
a: In a standard stream-static join, Databricks refreshes the static Delta table snapshot at each micro-batch — so in theory, today's merchants are available. The 2% miss pattern is a timing issue: the daily merchant load and the streaming micro-batch can race. If the micro-batch runs before the merchant pipeline finishes its MERGE/OVERWRITE, those new merchants aren't visible yet. Transactions for brand-new merchants processed in that window get a NULL enrichment and the join drops them. Fix strategies ranked by reliability: (1) Schedule the streaming job to start after the merchant pipeline completes (simplest, fragile); (2) Use a MERGE in foreachBatch against the Delta merchants table — the Spark driver re-reads the latest snapshot at each batch start, and your write can use WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT to fill the gap; (3) Keep a 'late enrichment' path — write unenriched rows to a quarantine table, then replay them once the merchant record is present. The production trap is assuming the join is purely a static read — the race between pipelines is the real bug.
fj: I'd add a fallback default value for missing merchants so the pipeline doesn't drop records.
fs: A NULL default masks a real data gap. The right fix is eliminating the race: either gate the stream start on the merchant load completing, or use a foreachBatch MERGE so the driver re-reads the merchants snapshot after each batch — not a null coalesce.

code.label: Race condition: static table read races merchant load
lines: # Stream-static join — snapshot may be stale
# if merchant pipeline races this micro-batch

lines: # Re-reads merchants at batch time, not job start
